I became so burnt out and numb my senior year of college in an education major program. My student teaching placement was a fight between me constantly feeling not good enough, numb, avoiding things that needed to get done, and then shamed by professors. And then I came out of college with that and just thinking I was shit and would always be shit. Not to mention I found out I had two undiagnosed chronic illnesses and somehow still felt like it was MY fault for being overwhelmed. I left teaching and realized no, I was EXHAUSTED and had been SHAMED for these cries for help. So, anyone going through this or anything- you are inherently worthy and are not defined by professions, grades, or employers/professors praise. You are wonderful ❤

I’m 24 years old, moved to America 3 years ago and got married to my best friend and took over the responsibilities of taking care of his elderly parents with Parkinson’s in there 80s and that was my wanting. I also live in a 5000sq feet house and have a ocd and want the house constantly clean. I also take care of three cats and 10 chicken including a garden full of veggies and flowers. I am also going to the gym recently to grow more muscle and stretch my body, thinking it would help with my overall health. I started getting gray hair lately and I now I’m totally burned out, but I have to push myself. I cook three meals a day for my parents in laws, low carb, low protein, high in veggies. My husband is at work and when he comes back he himself has a lot of stuff on his mind, i don’t want to dumb more stress on to him. Yet I feel like I am about to lose myself. The responsibility of being a full time caregiver and housewife is already hard and I also have to worry about political issues because I don’t have a green card. I don’t have any female friends, because I don’t have time to make any. My living space became my work environment, I constantly have to keep talking positiv “you can do it, push yourself, just gotta clean the cat toilette, take out the trash, feed the chicken, cook lunch, clean the kitchen, put away the groceries, clean out the pantry, feed the cats, check on parents in law, water the plant, repot the plants. if I push myself I get it all done in 4 hours and I can rest, I can do it, it isn’t that much” but who am I kidding, I can’t. I feel like I am all alone, I feel empty, I don’t feel happy anymore. Nothing is exciting, nothing is real and this isn’t even everything. There is so much more I have to deal with, I haven’t seen my family in 2 years since I can’t travel outside of the country until I get my green card. I am not suicidal saying this but I feel like I have lost the meaning to my life. I just exist. Maybe if I stop thinking of being a person and start living like a robot everything would be easier. I can’t really go anywhere for longer than 4 hours, afraid that my mother in law will get out of bed and fall, she is fall prone and has also dementia. Forget holidays, forget even taking off a day because I can’t find any care givers around me. I go sleep and wake up feeling like shit, I sometime go to bed without brushing my teeth and washing my face because those 5 minutes feel like an eternity. I Also have ADHD, my mind is constantly on a marathon. Anyways life is worth living, the sun will shine even after a bad storm. I guess I just have to keep pushing myself. Thankfully god gave me a bunch of hair, this world need to try twice as hard to turn all of it gray.

Lessions from PTBT: - It's better to set expectations for the work itself rather than for people. - Attract those who share the same ideals as you. Knowledge is necessary to understand what you want. - Always be ready to change yourself. Focus on the efficiency and outcomes of the work. - Enjoy the process; don't just focus on the results.

My boyfriend works a day job plus does community work within that job, but he's also a musician. He gets both physically and emotionally burnt out pretty easily, the one thing I see help him the most, is when he's on stage with his band. The weekends that he has shows, is practicing more often, and surrounded by people who share those hobbies/interests is when he's the most energetic, happiest, and healthiest looking. I know for a LOT of people it's really hard to have a hobby or indulge in interests and self care because their job takes a lot out of them and by the time they get home they can't do that, and it's hard to just "get a better job" but if you have an extra 15 minutes a day please pick up something small even if it's just reading a couple pages in a book you like or washing your face.

Having a dedicated "zero demands time" is a good one for people who have spent a long time in a high-stress situation, or in a relationship where your time wasn't valued. I spent many years like that and I'm still shocked when the weekend rolls around and I realise, "Hey, actually, I don't have to do anything in particular this weekend. I can do as much or as little as I'd like." No more overly demanding family and social expectations, no more constantly dealing with a partner's self-inflicted crisis, no more rushing to catch up on chores and errands, no more living on someone else's schedule, no more being made to feel guilty for trying to relax or enjoy a hobby after working a full-time job while studying a Bachelor's degree...😒 I actually have space to breathe, take my time, do things at a time that suits me, and not force myself to cram so much into so little time. That anxious feeling of needing to stay busy because that's what you're used to doesn't go away quickly (I still feel it!), but the more you remind yourself and follow through on "no obligations today!", it does start to fade, and you'll be surprised how much more control of your life you have.

Thay đổi bản thân giống như một vòng lặp mà em biết từ lúc bắt đầu đến khi kết thúc. Cảm giác như việc: mình rơi xuống đáy vực sâu, mong muốn được vùng dậy một cách mạnh mẽ và quyết liệt nhất, vì peer pressure, vì mọi người và các mối quan hệ xung quanh. Em luôn luôn đặt ra trong đầu mình một kì vọng và nguyện vọng nào đó, cố gắng chạy theo và mong muốn kết quả cuối cùng sẽ tốt đẹp. Nhưng càng trải qua nhiều vòng lặp về sự cứng ngắc trong cách thay đổi khiến em nhận ra rằng: Ồ, không ai giống ai cả, mỗi người đều có câu chuyện, cuộc đời và những bài học cho riêng mình, không thể vì một khuôn khổ "thay đổi bản thân" một cách chóng mà có thể cải thiện được những điểm yếu của chính mình. Đối với em ở thời điểm hiện tại, hiểu rõ con người mình đang thật sự muốn làm gì mới là chìa khóa tốt nhất mở ra con đường phù hợp nhất với bản thân. Lắng nghe đứa trẻ bên trong mình, trò chuyện và tâm sự với nó, thật lòng với những cảm xúc của bản thân. Và đặc biệt, đừng đặt áp lực qua những hình ảnh "lung linh" của người khác, họ giỏi họ xứng đáng với những ca ngợi và vinh danh, chúng ta không nên ganh tị để làm gì cả.

I myself had burnout early this year. Had to do the work of 3 people. Started having panic attacks, palpitations, brain fog, severe balance issues, severe hypertension, and dizziness. I was incapacitated for 2 months and I was in and out of work in that period. My mental health deteriorated and had suicidal ideation. It was a horrifying part of my life. Now Im on hypertension meds and my blood pressure back to normal. Work is now being delegated equally amongst members of the team. My mental health is now better since Im exercising and participating in group activities. Please remember that your mental wellbeing and health is paramount! Work is a transient part of your life and should not be the main focus of it.

i am diagnosed with ADHD and suspect i might be on the spectrum too, Altough I feel like I was always in a state of burnout since preschool, I suffered an extreme burnout for 5 years between 13-18(used to think it was just puberty). I was dissociative majority of the time so I barely retained any information or memories. EVERYTHING was complicated and tiring for me but i couldn't ask for help because that would make me "incompetent". I was failing miserably at school and didn't understand my peers so i ignored them, barely got out of bed so i got physically weak and was malnourished most of the time because food was also complicated(i only ate things i was hyperfixated on). I did eventually slowly get out of that stage thanks to my best friend that basically adopted me and helped me come in terms with my needs and became my safety net. But to think that only if my parents had the awareness you try to share... Thank you for being a voice for us no matter how far it reaches

But I mourn it because I genuinely loved giving my blood, sweat, and tears to performance and I've tried so many other paths, but they all result in burnout from knowing what my purpose is but not having the connections or wealth to play around and be curious with it full-time, or even part-time.

Man this hits hard. I work a 8-5 job but most days I start at 5am and end at 11pm! So I'm just in a bad cycle of: I don't finish work because I'm tired and not rested. So i work on weekends to catch up. And then comes the week again where I'm tired and unrested. Then work through the weekend again to finish. And it's not healthy.

really liked the points you made about how sitting and scrolling is NOT resting. i know i have made that mistake plenty of times: "ugh, I had a miserable shift, can't wait to go home and relax by just sitting on my beanbag chair with some snacks and my phone" but then scrolling on my phone doesn't relax me in the slightest, it just DISTRACTS me and that's not the same at all. i recently deleted insta and x the everything app (formerly known as twitter) so now i'm trying to get away from staring into my nightmare rectangle for hours a day. we'll see how it goes, wish me luck lol

This is a great talk. I'm not sure how it showed up on my feed, but for 6 years (2007-2013) I dealt with one medical emergency after another in my family and took care of my mom as she had ovarian cancer. After that I had a cancer scare (2016) and surgery that left me with a paralyzed vocal cord. And alongside it all, for the last 9 years (2013-2022) I have been working on major projects under intense deadlines and with excessive overtime (like 50%+), with little reward. A couple of years ago I made a plan to move to a mountain town. I currently live in a small apartment and live quite frugally overall to make that dream a reality. We know what real estate has done these last two years, so as a single person I feel I need work twice as hard to make it all happen. For several years my cortisol levels have been sky-high (never coming down in any of the testing I've had done), and created autoimmune issues. And yet, here I am, continuing to work so I can take care of myself when I'm older (I'm in my 40s now). And with Covid an overseas vacation has been put off for the third year in a row. Is this burnout? Does it take longer to get through it the longer you've experienced it?

coisas de casa, me sinto muito pior por isso e eu quando tento pensar, raciocinal ou me lembrar de alguma coisa a minha cabeça entra em parafuso eu fico como estou agora com a perda da força das minhas pernas e tremor na mãos e fico tremendo a casa com a cabeça e o corpo todo tremendo como se eu tivesse mal de Parkinson 😢......Doutora eu sofri muito perturbação da família do meu marido, e a 4 anos atrás meu cunhado entrou na minha casa cheio de si, aí eu falei q não queria ele entrando na minha casa, e ele começou a me xingar e a minha filha veio me defender e ele colocou a faca no pescoço da minha filha, e a minha cabeça entrou em parafuso depois disso, e esses sintomas q eu tenho hoje q a outra psiquiatra q me tratava disse q eu tenho é uma depressão severa .......Doutora eu não trabalho fora, eu cuidava do meu lar, marido e filhos e à anos eu não consigo cuidar mais do meu lar 😢, qm cuida mais ou menos é meu marido e minha filha......o q me fez voltar a ter essas crises fortes novamente de andar com as pernas bambas e não conseguir mais andar sozinha sem ter alguém me segurando, o meu Marido tem um filho viciado em drogas q tbm toma antidepressivos q está roubando tudo na minha casa, e eu falo com o meu marido, meu marido briga com ele e não quer q eu impeça o filho dele de entrar na minha casa.......tenho um filho deficiente mental e esquizofrênico q passa o dia inteiro me falando os pensamentos terríveis q ele tem, e os pensamentos mandam ele fazer coisas terríveis e um neto de 11 anos q tá na pré aborrecencia q mora comigo desde q nasceu, e tá muito teimoso e abusado, e eu quero q ele vá embora morar com a mãe dele, e eu tô à mais de uma semana implorando pra ela vir buscar ele só pra passar pelo menos o resto das férias escolares com ela, e ela não fala nada, e me manda mensagem e nem toca no assunto filho dele......então Doutora Maria Fernanda e esse o motivo de eu estar com cansaço mental e ainda estar tendo crises de ansiedade como essa q me levou pro hospital hoje, pressão arterial descontrolada, glicose descontrolada, aceleração do coração e perda do controle do meu corpo com essa fraqueza física q não me deixa andar sozinha a não ser segurada por outros......Doutora Maria Fernanda estou me sentindo a ponto de enlouquecer, me sinto profundamente triste, sem vida, e sem ânimo pra nada, com uma forte vontade de chutar tudo pro alto e morrer, tô tento crises de choro e desabei no consultório da médica da emergência 😢......e o entiado drogado foi parar no hospital esses dias com água no pulmão, com a cabeça sangrando pq caiu no meio da ria e tava todo entubado desacordado, aí hoje eu torcendo pra ele morrer logo, ele acordou e já queria sair do hospital e foi rancando tudo q tava ligado nele, me perdoem por eu dizer q eu queria q ele estivesse morto é pq ele vivendo o meu sofrimento vai piorar 😭.......amanhã eu tenho q levar o meu filho pro psiquiatra, e a consulta dele é no Méier a tarde, e a filha e a ex mulher do meu marido querem q ele vá buscar elas novamente lá na casa dela, pra ficar lá esperando elas verem ele pq eles já foram hoje, ainda querem ir amanhã 😢......ele teve que me levar pra emergência e depois ir na casa delas pra levar pro hospital, aí depois de um bom tempo q eu fiquei lá no hospital ele foi lá me buscar, me deixou aqui em casa é voltou novamente pro hospital pra daí levar elas pra casa e vir pra casa, mas ele ainda não parou aqui perto de mim ainda......e é isso Doutora, tô a ponto de endoidar e a vontade de me matar é muito grande sabe, mas tenho q pensar no meu filho 😭 me desculpe pq eu falo muito 😢

Thank you for covering this. I am experiencing this in a major way. Caregivers, especially those taking care of a parent or child, who lack support are perfect candidates for burnout. When you are a 24/7 carer, it's not sustainable. People assume that because you're at home you're "taking time off" to be a carer, and assume you have all of this time off/to yourself because you aren't in the workforce. I became a single parent to a 6wo at the beginning of the lockdown, due to DV. I had a very high powered career and had to drop every ambition I had. I have to take care of my daughter 24/7/365. I cannot bathe by myself, sleep when I want/need to, or really doing anything when I want or need to. It's thrust me into a bad financial situation, because I receive no support from the father. Nevermind the fact that I am reeling from DV and 2+ years of being dragged through the courts, and several bogus formal CPS reports against me. I have some serious health issues that I cannot address because I cannot attend to because I cannot bring a toddler to medical and other appointments. Finding a babysitter is extremely competitive where I live, and costs $25-30+ hour. Even if I'm working at $75/hour, more than half ends up going to childcare- if I can get someone. I have experienced burnout in the workplace, but this is beyond that. The chronic exhaustion has been causing cognitive decline. I am forgetful and can hardly focus. I've reached out for help, but because due to several reasons I am not able to qualify for any outside assistance. I was in therapy until a year ago, but my therapist told me we had worked through everything she was able to help me with. I'm tired of people telling me that I need to seek medical or mental health treatment. I have, and I know that I can benefit from both. But given certain circumstances, they won't help. I need help and support- mainly in taking care of my child so I can do what I need to take care of myself. So if you know a caregiver struggling to take care of a young child or parent, offer to step in to lessen the load. Even just a couple hours of help can make a huge difference.

I love my job. It isn't my dream job, but that's why I'm going to school again. But I genuinely enjoy my job when I think about it. However, there have been many, many days when I've been headed to work and thought, "God, I hope I break my leg on the way. I would rather break my leg than go to work today," or, "I wouldn't even care if a car hit me right now. I wouldn't have to go to work if a car hit me." Which are alarming reactions for having to go to work. I feel guilty about it, or disingenuous, because I know I like my work. I love making people's day better, I love coffee, I even love cleaning. But god damn if I don't dread going more days than not lately.

Thank you for this video, I guess the universe is telling me enough is enough it's time to reset and start living life to the fullest. I lost my mom when I was 9 years old. Then my dad passed away when I was 12 years old. The passing of my dad was the hardest because he was my place of protection and comfort. My paternal family took turns to take care of me and my younger brother. I became a troublesome teenager. I met my husband and got married at 19yrs my husband's family did not accept me. There were quarrels now and again in the house. We had our firstborn when I was almost 21 years old, and now we have 4 kids. I don't even know how to put what I've been through in words. In short, it was mentally draining, and it led me into a deep depression. Our 2nd born is deaf she had meningitis at 8 months old. My husband's family accused me of being a witch and that I used some charms, which was the reason for my daughter's deafness. I tried to be strong for my marriage and my kids because my husband was deeply hurt when he heard what his family thought of me. Time passed, and my marriage didn't feel like what it used to be. My husband was my best friend. I could tell him anything no matter how silly. Most time of my marriage, we were staying apart because my husband worked as an Expart. Late last year, he got a job in Europe, and it gave us the opportunity to relocate and start staying together. So he later apologized for his behavior, saying he was also adjusting to having us around, and the kid's noise was not making it easy for him to adjust. But thankfully, he is back to being the man that I married. But the severe stress that I constantly had led me to a place I sometimes don't even recognize myself. I don't have friends, I don't have any motivation, I'm always weak, I have an eating disorder, difficulty falling asleep, all of the disorders mentioned in this video. I'm now 38, and I want a new happy self.

Thank you for this amazing episode, Professor. It has so much value. 💐 Only, I found interesting nutrition advice. Recommending starchy carbs at night isn’t very common. I usually suggest eating most carbs earlier in the day for the following reasons: 1. After sunset, insulin sensitivity tends to decrease, making carbohydrate uptake slightly less efficient. 2. During nocturnal fasting, blood sugar regulation is generally better when dinner is rich in protein and fiber. 3. Since we’re preparing for sleep, the immediate need for quick energy is relatively low — we benefit more from that active energy during the day rather than at night.

In teachers college we discussed procrastination. The idea of “moderate procrastination” came up that struck a cord with me - if you start engaging in a topic (an assignment in my case) and then let it go for a week or two, you’re going to be thinking about it and coming up with ideas even if you’re not actually working on it, so that when you DO sit down to create a product, much of the mental work is already done. Reminded me of your “mental composting”.

Lessons from the video: How to overcome depression and fear during covid-19? Don't think about the past and future, the past is something you can't change, what about the future you don't worry about when it hasn't happened yet, focus on the present and actions that create value, if you're still breathing, still alive, seeing your family means you're lucky.

Lessons from this video: 1. Forgive yourself and accept that you are human—capable of making mistakes, experiencing pain, and getting hurt. This not only lightens your heart but also lays the foundation for loving and taking care of yourself. 2. Suffering is a natural part of being human; it shouldn’t be dismissed or hastily covered up. Taking the time to face and feel the pain is essential for healing. 3. Instead of seeking empathy from others, be honest with yourself and embrace your true emotions. True healing comes from within and does not depend on external advice or comfort. 4. Small steps, such as walking, exercising, or practicing self-care, can lead to significant changes. These positive habits gradually strengthen both your body and mind. 5. Everyone has their own struggles, and suffering is not unusual. Acknowledging it as a part of life helps reduce feelings of inferiority or self-blame. 6. Learn to love and care for yourself even when you feel hurt or weak. This self-love will carry you through challenging times. 7. Stop blaming circumstances or others and take responsibility for your actions and emotions. Only by doing so can you truly change.

Beautifully stated and great observations and points. I’ve experienced “big traumas” and plenty of day to day in childhood and adulthood. Even when I’m high on my career and ambitious, I’m equally floored and burnt out. I have felt burnt out for many many many many years. Now I have kids and want this to shift for so much more than myself. It’s getting there but still have some ways to go. What I always loved was music but I was never “the best” at it. I certainly wasn’t good enough to go to school for it so I followed what made me feel competent and strong and smart, unlike anything before and that was health and wellness and public health. I do love it and it’s near to my heart but I think there is a core much deeper I need to remember and stop being afraid to explore and try to enjoy again.

Cô gái trong MV thật đáng thương. Có lẽ cô ấy đang cô đơn sau chia tay. Đầu MV cô ấy cố dành những khoảng thời gian cho bản thân làm những việc cô thích, cố gắng sống vui tươi như quên đi cái sự cô đơn trống vắng. Nhưng thực ra cô không thể quên anh ấy và luôn giữ hình ảnh của anh ấy ở mọi nơi mọi lúc. Có thể đó là những nơi, những khoảng thời gian cô ấy và anh ấy yêu nhau, cũng có thể cô ấy muốn mang anh ấy đi đến nơi mà cô ấy và anh ấy hứa hẹn mà chưa từng tới. Cô điên dại, bộc lộ mọi cảm xúc tình yêu với hình bóng lạnh lùng ấy. Tuy nhiên tình yêu của cô dành anh ấy bị ngăn cách bởi 1 tủ kính vô hình, rất mong manh nhưng cô không thể ôm chầm lấy anh ấy, đưa tận tay bông hoa cho anh ấy. Tình yêu của cô chỉ được đáp lại bằng sự lạnh giá từ anh ấy. Có thể do họ giận hờn nhau, có thể anh ấy đã hết yêu cô ấy, cũng có thể anh ấy đã có mối tình mới với một cô gái khác. Tất cả điều đó làm cô gái vỡ oà trong cơn mưa. Khi quay trở lại căn phòng mới là lúc cô ấy thực sự cảm nhận được cô độc trong con tim mình 😭

This is a great critique on administrative fluff, but this is not burn out. There was a time in my life when meetings and emails were my only "rest stop" for the day. Burnout is when you're working 3 different jobs too afford your bills because you have no other choice. Burnout is when you have exhausted your physical and mental bandwidth to depletion and your brain and body put you in a time out.

Im abused physically and mentally in my childhood. I was compared to other kids and my siblings, which made me look small and good for nothing. Now Im 46 and suffer from severe migraine that started from age 16 . I have no confidence to do anything. Always scared. Always worried. Always fearful. Things others do so effortlessly is a big deal for me.. Finding it difficult to face every day..every situation. Im a joke to many. People laugh at me and ask me sarcastically if there's anything in the world im not scared of. I dont know what to do . I feel I do not fit in this world. Im finding it so difficult. With the help of your videos, im trying to move forward in life. Thank you for making such awesome videos.
